overview.description: The Mendri Ghumar Waterfalls (Hindi: मेंद्री घूमर) is a seasonal and natural waterfall located at around 44 kilometres (27 mi) to the west of Jagdalpur, in Bastar district in the Indian state of Chhattisgarh. It is very close to Chitrakote and Tamda Ghumar Waterfalls.

topPlaces.name: Narayanpal Temple
topPlaces.description: The Narayanpal Temple is a historic Hindu temple located near the confluence of the Indravati and Narangi rivers in the Bastar district of Chhattisgarh, India. It is dedicated to the Hindu god Vishnu, the protector of the universe in Hindu mythology.

items.note: The Narayanpal Temple is a historic Hindu temple located near the confluence of the Indravati and Narangi rivers in the Bastar district of Chhattisgarh, India. It is dedicated to the Hindu god Vishnu, the protector of the universe in Hindu mythology.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Mendri Ghumar?
faq.a: The best time to visit Mendri Ghumar is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Mendri Ghumar?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Swami Vivekananda Airport, Raipur (224 km) and the nearest railway station is Raipur Junction (232 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Mendri Ghumar?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Mendri Ghumar at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Mendri Ghumar?
faq.a: Stays in Mendri Ghumar start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Mendri Ghumar?
faq.a: The top attractions are Narayanpal Temple.
